response: The internet originated from US Department of Defense research in the 1960s, with ARPANET's first message sent in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 establishing its modern foundation. Tim Berners-Lee's invention of the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989, followed by the Mosaic browser in 1993, made the internet accessible to the public, fueling the dot-com boom and the rise of giants like Amazon and Google. Subsequent milestones—social media in the mid-2000s, the 2007 iPhone launch, and AWS's 2006 debut—made internet access mobile and cloud-based. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underlies nearly every facet of modern life.
